findings.name: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
findings.description: The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controllable input before it is placed in output that is used as a web page that is served to other users.
findings.evidence: src/main/resources/static/js/upload.js:160 — createPhotoCard() embeds photo.originalFileName directly into HTML via insertAdjacentHTML without escaping; showErrors() also uses innerHTML with unescaped filenames from server response

findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Password
findings.description: The product contains a hard-coded password, which it uses for its own inbound authentication or for outbound communication to external components.

findings.name: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
findings.description: The product allows the upload or transfer of dangerous file types that are automatically processed within its environment.
findings.evidence: src/main/java/com/photoalbum/service/impl/PhotoServiceImpl.java:74 — Validates file type using client-supplied file.getContentType() only; no server-side magic byte verification. Attacker can spoof MIME type header to bypass restriction.

findings.name: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
findings.description: The product specifies permissions for a security-critical resource in a way that allows that resource to be read or modified by unintended actors.
findings.evidence: src/main/resources/application.properties — File permissions -rw-rw-r-- (world-readable, group-writable) on configuration file containing plaintext database credentials

findings.name: Insufficient Logging
findings.description: When a security-critical event occurs, the product either does not record the event or omits important details about the event when logging it.
findings.evidence: No authentication or authorization mechanism exists; security-critical operations (delete, upload) log action outcomes but no user identity/session, no failed authentication events, no access control violations are ever logged

findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
findings.description: The product contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key.
findings.evidence: src/main/resources/application.properties:12-13 and src/main/resources/application-docker.properties:2-3 — Both files contain hardcoded database username 'photoalbum' and password 'photoalbum' in plaintext
